title: SherlockInvestigation
description: Complete snapshot of a Sherlock falsification-driven investigation. Returned by GPT-5.6 on every iteration. The client computes diffs between consecutive snapshots to visualize confidence changes, graveyard entries and revivals. ID conventions: evidence E1..En, hypotheses H1..Hn, anomalies A1..An, missing evidence M1..Mn, matrix items X1..Xn.

schema_version.description: Version of this schema, e.g. '1.0.0'. Allows the client to reject incompatible payloads.

case_id.description: Stable identifier for the case, provided by the client and echoed back unchanged.

case_title.description: Short human-readable title for the investigation, e.g. 'Checkout 500 errors 23:10-23:40'.

iteration.description: Investigation iteration number. 1 = baseline analysis; >=2 means new evidence was added and prior state was re-evaluated.

domain.description: Free-text domain label, e.g. 'IT incident', 'industrial QC', 'finance'. Informative only; the engine is domain-independent.

observed_outcome.description: What actually happened, as declared by the user. Verbatim or lightly normalized; never reinterpreted.

expected_behavior.description: What normal behavior should have looked like, as declared by the user. This is the ONLY anchor for 'expected but absent' items: omissions must be derivable from this declaration, never invented.

evidence.description: All evidence items known in this iteration, both user-provided and added in later iterations.

id.description: Unique evidence id, format 'E<number>'. Stable across iterations.

label.description: Short name, e.g. 'Deploy log', 'TLS renewal cron log'.

content.description: The evidence content or a faithful summary of it. No interpretation here; interpretation lives in hypothesis links.

provided_in_iteration.description: Iteration in which this evidence entered the case. Enables the client to highlight new evidence.

expectation_matrix.description: The 2x2 falsification matrix: expected/unexpected x present/absent. Every item must be classifiable into exactly one quadrant.

expected_present.description: Quadrant 1 - What happened normally: events that were expected and did occur. Baseline sanity; usually low investigative value.

unexpected_present.description: Quadrant 2 - What appeared and should not be there: unexpected events that did occur. Classic anomaly territory.

expected_absent.description: Quadrant 3 - THE KILLER QUADRANT. What should have appeared and did not: the dog that didn't bark. Each item MUST be justified by case.expected_behavior; the engine must never invent expectations not grounded in the declared normal behavior.

unexpected_absent.description: Quadrant 4 - Absences that rule out or weaken hypotheses: things a given hypothesis would have produced, whose absence discriminates. Each item should reference the hypotheses it weakens.

anomalies.description: First-class contradictions. An investigation starts with at least one anomaly. One anomaly can spawn multiple hypotheses.

id.description: Unique anomaly id, format 'A<number>'. Stable across iterations.

description.description: The contradiction stated plainly: what clashes with what.

matrix_item_ids.description: Ids of expectation_matrix items that constitute this anomaly.

severity.description: Investigative weight of the anomaly. NOTE: a small unexplained anomaly can outweigh a large partially-explained one; severity reflects discriminating power, not size.

fully_explained.description: True only if the current prime suspect accounts for this anomaly completely. Unexplained anomalies drive open_case_index up.

hypothesis_ids.description: Hypotheses generated by or relevant to this anomaly.

hypotheses.description: All hypotheses ever considered, in every state. The Hypothesis Graveyard is the subset with status 'rejected'; it is a client-side view, not a separate entity. Ordered by confidence descending among active ones.

id.description: Unique hypothesis id, format 'H<number>'. Stable across iterations; never reuse ids.

statement.description: The hypothesis as a falsifiable claim.

origin.description: Whether the hypothesis was proposed by the user or generated by the engine.

status.description: Lifecycle state. 'weakened': seriously damaged but not eliminated. 'rejected': in the Graveyard; requires killed_by and resurrection_condition. 'revived': previously rejected, brought back by new evidence matching its resurrection_condition; must never revive without such evidence.

confidence.description: Current confidence 0-100. Must DECREASE when the hypothesis fails to produce effects it should have produced (expected_but_absent_ids non-empty and unexplained).

supported_by.description: Evidence in favor, each with the reasoning link.

contradicted_by.description: Evidence against, each with the reasoning link.

expected_but_absent_ids.description: Ids of missing_evidence items this hypothesis SHOULD have produced. Non-empty means the hypothesis predicts effects that are not observed.

would_be_refuted_by.description: Mandatory self-refutation clause: which concrete datum would prove this hypothesis wrong. Every hypothesis must be falsifiable or it does not belong here.

killed_by.description: Only for status 'rejected': the evidence id or datum that killed it. Null otherwise.

resurrection_condition.description: Only for status 'rejected': the concrete condition under which it may return. Prevents dead explanations from resurfacing by wishful thinking. Null otherwise.

missing_evidence.description: The WALD section: data that should exist and is not available, classified by discriminating power.

description.description: What is missing and where it should exist, anchored to case.expected_behavior or to a hypothesis prediction.

criticality.description: 'critical': could close or reopen the case. 'useful': would shift confidences. 'noise': would change little.

impact_if_found.description: How the interpretation would change if this evidence appeared, in both directions where relevant.

related_hypothesis_ids.description: Hypotheses whose confidence this missing evidence affects.

prime_suspect.description: The single strongest current explanation, with its conviction and absolution conditions. Must correspond to the active hypothesis with highest confidence.

justification.description: Why it leads: which anomalies it explains that rivals do not, and which absences still count against it.

condemning_datum.description: The concrete datum that would confirm it.

absolving_datum.description: The concrete datum that would clear it.

coherence.description: How well the current best explanation accounts for the full picture. Score without explanation is forbidden: metrics must not be opaque.

score.description: 0 = the leading explanation contradicts or ignores most of the picture; 100 = fully causally coherent.

explanation.description: What a rational observer expected, what actually happened, and why the score is what it is.

open_case_index.description: How much evidence remains unexplained (formerly 'Poirot index'). Measures unexplained evidence count and weight, not anomaly size: two small unexplained anomalies can outweigh one large partially-explained one.

score.description: 0 = nearly everything is explained; 100 = large amount of evidence does not fit any current hypothesis.

explanation.description: Which specific items remain unexplained and why they keep the case open.

next_test.description: The single observation or experiment with the highest discriminating power between the top competing hypotheses. Sherlock is an investigator, not an oracle: this is its main output.

description.description: The concrete test, observation or datum to obtain next.

discriminates_between.description: Hypothesis ids this test separates. Minimum two: a test that discriminates nothing is not a test.

outcome_map.description: What each possible result would mean.

observed_result.description: A possible outcome of the test.

favors_hypothesis_id.description: Hypothesis this outcome strengthens, or null if it strengthens none.

weakens_hypothesis_id.description: Hypothesis this outcome weakens, or null if it weakens none.

mirror_question.description: The MIRROR: literally answer the question 'If in one week we discovered our prime suspect was false, which clue are we probably ignoring today?'. One question or short reflection; keeps the investigator honest.

learning.description: Bayesian-style update relative to the previous iteration. Present from iteration 1, where it acts as baseline.

is_baseline.description: True only on iteration 1: establishes initial confidences with no prior to compare against.

summary.description: Which hypothesis gains, which loses, which holds, and which evidence changed our mind. On baseline: why initial confidences are set as they are.

updates.description: Per-hypothesis deltas. On baseline, previous_* fields are null.

previous_confidence.description: Confidence in previous iteration; null on baseline.

previous_status.description: Status in previous iteration; null on baseline.

reason.description: The specific evidence or absence that caused the change. 'No change' is a valid reason.

description.description: The event or absence, stated concretely.

evidence_ids.description: Supporting evidence ids. May be empty for absences (quadrants 3 and 4), where the point is precisely that no evidence exists.

significance.description: Why this item matters to the investigation.

related_hypothesis_ids.description: Hypotheses this item supports or weakens.

evidence_id.description: Id of the linked evidence item.

reason.description: The reasoning: why this evidence supports or contradicts the hypothesis. The Evidence -> supports/weakens -> reading format lives here.
